event: diagnosed with active methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA) mitral valve acute bacterial endocarditis
admitted for intravenous antibiotic treatment for pyelonephritis
diagnosed with disseminated intravascular coagulation with sepsis
three sets of blood cultures within 24 h of admission grew MSSA
recombinant human soluble thrombomodulin not used
risk of maternal and fetal death
minimally invasive thoracoscopic mitral valve repair
routine evaluation of fetal heart rate showed no abnormalities
rupture of chordae tendineae of A3 segment
transvaginal ultrasound for fetal heart rate regularly
two sets of blood cultures 5 days postoperatively no bacterial growth
anesthetic agents generally safe during pregnancy
